symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic burning/gnawing epigastric pain, worse shortly after meals, with night waking

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Bleeding peptic ulcer / upper gastrointestinal haemorrhage
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Meal-related epigastric pain is the classic chronic background of peptic ulcer disease.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Bleeding peptic ulcer / upper gastrointestinal haemorrhage
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Altered (coffee-ground) blood is direct evidence of active upper GI bleeding; patient/caregiver-observable so admissible in telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Bleeding peptic ulcer / upper gastrointestinal haemorrhage
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Digested blood per rectum indicates a significant upper GI bleed; history-reportable and, when observed by the caregiver, video/photo-corroborable.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unintentional weight loss (~4 kg) and early satiety

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Weight loss with early satiety is an upper GI alarm feature mandating endoscopic malignancy exclusion.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Bleeding peptic ulcer / upper gastrointestinal haemorrhage
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Postural lightheadedness is a patient-reportable proxy for hypovolaemia from ongoing blood loss.

symptoms.symptom_name: Orthostatic hypotension (BP 95/60 supine, 80/50 standing) with tachycardia (HR ~115)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Bleeding peptic ulcer / upper gastrointestinal haemorrhage
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective haemodynamic compromise / early haemorrhagic shock — sealed clinician measurement; the AI infers circulatory compromise from reported presyncope, not a measured BP.

symptoms.symptom_name: Epigastric tenderness on deep palpation without board-like rigidity or rebound; hyperactive bowel sounds; melaena on digital rectal exam

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Bleeding peptic ulcer / upper gastrointestinal haemorrhage
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Localised epigastric tenderness fits peptic ulcer; the ABSENCE of peritonism argues against free perforation — but this requires a clinician's hands and cannot be assessed remotely, so perforation is not excludable via telehealth.
No board-like rigidity or rebound on examination makes free perforation less likely — a clinician-only finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pallor and diaphoresis with delayed capillary refill

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Bleeding peptic ulcer / upper gastrointestinal haemorrhage
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Peripheral signs of shock; pallor and sweating are partly video-observable, but capillary refill and true perfusion require in-person assessment.

symptoms.symptom_name: Untreated prior positive Helicobacter pylori test; no NSAID use

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Bleeding peptic ulcer / upper gastrointestinal haemorrhage
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Untreated H. pylori is a leading cause of peptic ulcer; absence of NSAID use shifts aetiology toward H. pylori.

symptoms.symptom_name: Haemoglobin / full blood count, urea and coagulation profile

investigation_details.investigation_name: Full blood count, urea and electrolytes, coagulation profile, group and hold/crossmatch
investigation_details.expected_result: Anaemia and a raised urea:creatinine ratio typical of an upper GI bleed (hospital bloods)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Degree of blood loss and transfusion need cannot be quantified; requires venepuncture unavailable in telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Bleeding peptic ulcer / upper gastrointestinal haemorrhage
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Quantifies blood loss and guides resuscitation — a hospital investigation, never available in real-time telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Upper GI endoscopy showing the bleeding mucosal defect (ulcer) and excluding malignancy

investigation_details.expected_result: A mucosal defect (peptic ulcer) with an identifiable bleeding point; biopsy to exclude malignancy and test for H. pylori
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The bleeding source is neither controlled nor characterised, and malignancy is not excluded; endoscopy is a hospital procedure.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Bleeding peptic ulcer / upper gastrointestinal haemorrhage
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Endoscopy is the confirmatory and therapeutic test — never achievable via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Active upper GI bleed with haemodynamic compromise
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Bleeding peptic ulcer / upper gastrointestinal haemorrhage
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Coffee-ground emesis + melaena + postural presyncope together define a time-critical upper GI bleed requiring immediate 000 escalation — all three are patient/caregiver-reportable.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency hospital assessment with IV access, bloods (FBC/U&E/coag/crossmatch) and urgent upper GI endoscopy
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Active upper GI haemorrhage with haemodynamic compromise requires in-hospital resuscitation and urgent endoscopic haemostasis — arranged via 000, not achievable in telehealth.
